response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer with broad language exposure, but he misses nearly every hard requirement for this role: no native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German), no demonstrated C-for-UNIX depth beyond a brief C/C++ IDE-tooling role, no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded network-security development. The Japanese language requirement alone is disqualifying, and the technical profile is oriented toward web/product engineering rather than embedded firmware.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/low-level work
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) at Sociomantic
Strong craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese proficiency, a stated native-level requirement
No Linux kernel modification, switch/networking, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
C experience is minimal and dated; career centers on TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Solid general engineering experience and testing discipline
